title: How Justice Clock India estimates Supreme Court time-to-judgment
question: How can public judgment metadata support historical estimated time-to-judgment research?
shortAnswer: The first draft explains the estimation method and its limitations. It does not publish unsupported headline numbers.
methodology: Compare judgment year against the strongest available start-year signal, preferring exact filing or registration dates where available and structured case or diary year otherwise.
limitations: Exact filing-to-disposal dates are not always available in public metadata. Estimates are excluded when a credible start-year signal is missing.
sourceNotes: Supreme Court of India public judgment metadata
NJDG public dashboard snapshots where manually captured
